commit.message:
Fix AI Review workflow exit-128 failures from merge-conflict cleanup errors (#671)

* Initial plan

* Fix merge conflict detection: only move untracked files to MERGE_STASH, make cleanup non-fatal

Root cause: the MERGE_STASH loop moved entire directories (including tracked
files like scripts/gh_helpers.sh) out of the working tree, causing git merge
to fail with "Entry ... not uptodate" and git merge --abort to exit 128.

Changes:
- MERGE_STASH loop now checks git ls-files for tracked content:
  - Entirely-untracked dirs are moved as a whole (existing behaviour)
  - Dirs with tracked files only have their untracked files moved individually,
    preserving full relative-path structure to avoid subdirectory collisions
- git merge --abort calls in both no-conflict and conflict branches are now
  `|| true` so cleanup never hard-fails the job
- No-conflict branch also runs git reset --hard HEAD || true and
  git clean -fd || true as an additional safety net
- Comments added explaining why cleanup is best-effort and why the conflict
  path exits non-fatally (exit 0)

commit.message:
fix: crypto-side playtest — BUG-041–048 (NFT minting, loot tiers, CAPS economy, scrap lock) (#217)

* Initial plan

* fix: crypto-side playtest bugs BUG-041 through BUG-048

BUG-041 (High) - mint-item.js: Replace non-atomic GET+SET daily-limit
check with atomic redis.incr() + expire() + decr-on-exceed to eliminate
TOCTOU race where concurrent requests could bypass the mint daily cap.

BUG-042 (High) - Tiered loot CAPS economy non-functional:
- loot-voucher.js: Calculate CAPS reward from POI rarity (RARITY_CAPS
  map with env-configurable values per tier) and sign it into the voucher
  as voucher.caps so the redemption amount is server-authenticated.
- gps.js: Include voucher.caps in serializeVoucherMessage (backward-
  compatible: old vouchers without caps field still verify correctly).
- redeem-voucher.js: Use voucher.caps directly (covered by NaCl
  signature) instead of broken LOOT_ID_TO_CAPS comma-delimited parser.

BUG-043 (Medium) - loot-voucher.js: Expand randomInt lootId range from
1,000,000 to 2^48 to dramatically reduce birthday-paradox PDA seed
collision probability for on-chain ClaimLoot.

BUG-044 (Medium) - redeem-voucher.js: Pre-call redis.key() in
VOUCHER_USED_KEY so voucher replay-protection keys are stored at
afw:afw:voucher:used:* (double-prefix, consistent with all other
app keys) instead of afw:voucher:used:* (orphaned single-prefix).

BUG-046 (Low) - caps.js: Add capsBalanceLimiter (30 req/min) to
GET /:wallet to prevent bulk wallet enumeration without authentication.

BUG-047 (Low) - player-state.js + dungeon.js: Add MAX_CAPS = 999_999_999
ceiling to awardCaps() and dungeon loot assignment to match backend limit
and prevent Pip-Boy display overflow.

BUG-048 (Low) - scrap-nft.js: Use per-NFT lock key (wallet+nftMint)
instead of per-wallet lock so concurrent scraps of different NFTs are
no longer unnecessarily serialized with 15-second waits.

Add 11 regression tests (BUG-041..048) — total test count: 94 → 105.

feat(peer-review): v1.5 — evals 24-28, clarity fixes, reviewer model note (#94)

## Summary

- **5 new evals (A)**: evals 24–28 cover previously untested scenarios —
re-scan y-response, PR URL output, unsupported model error, branch not
found, and sub-model splitting. Eval 28 (`submodel-splitting`)
discriminates at +0.33 (severity normalization `medium→major` fails
without skill). Headline delta moves from +29% to +26% as 3 of 5 new
evals are non-discriminating (eval 26 contaminated/excluded from
aggregates).
- **SKILL.md clarity (B)**: path target section now specifies recursive
traversal and skips binaries/large files. The 3× repeated PR URL rule in
Step 6 is consolidated into one authoritative statement at the top of
the section.
- **Reviewer model note (C)**: Step 1 now explains why `claude-opus-4-6`
is the default (quality > cost — a cheaper model might miss real

commit.message:
fix: lagre forsinket hendelse med opprinnelig endretTid istedenfor bump (#765)

* fix: lagre forsinket hendelse med opprinnelig endretTid istedenfor bump

Tidligere ble hendelser med eldre endretTid enn siste lagrede rad bumped
(+1µs etter forrige rad), noe som plasserte den utdaterte hendelsen som
nyeste tilstand i BigQuery. Dette ga feil gjeldende status.

Ny logikk splitter de to tilfellene:
- Samme endretTid (==): behold bump (+1µs) for samtidige hendelser
- Eldre endretTid (>): lagre med opprinnelig tidsstempel, slik at
  hendelsen havner på riktig historisk posisjon og gjeldende tilstand
  (høyeste endretTid) forblir korrekt. Ingen statuser (f.eks. AVSLUTTET,
  IVERKSETTES) går tapt.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: idempotens for gjentagende stale retries + enklere direktetest

Problem: dersom en forsinket retry-jobb kjørte to ganger, ble hendelsen
lagret dobbelt. hentSisteHendelseForBehandling peker alltid på den
nyeste raden (ORDER BY endret_tid DESC), så duplikatsjekken mot siste
misset den allerede-lagrede stale-raden.

Løsning:
- Legg til hentHendelseMedEndretTid(uuid, endretTid) i
  SakstatistikkRepository/Impl
- I tilpassEndretTid: ved eldre endretTid, sjekk om det allerede
  finnes en rad med samme (uuid, endretTid) som er duplikat av den
  innkommende hendelsen; hopp over hvis ja
- Trekk ut tilpassEndretTid som privat hjelpemetode (forkorter
  lagreBQBehandling under detekt-grensen)

Test:
- Ny direktetest verifiserer at stale hendelse lagres med opprinnelig
  endretTid og at andre retry av identisk hendelse hoppes over

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fiks idempotenssjekk for resending med samme endretTid

hentHendelseMedEndretTid filtrerer nå på erResending slik at
en resending-hendelse ikke feilaktig hoppes over fordi en
ikke-resending rad allerede finnes med samme endretTid.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Legg til ORDER BY teknisk_tid DESC i hentHendelseMedEndretTid

Sikrer deterministisk oppførsel dersom flere rader har samme
(behandling_uuid, endret_tid, er_relast).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat/variables4: Local Glyphs in Builders (#363)

* [backend] minor docstring update

* feat: add local glyphs to BlueprintBuilder

Adds a third glyph type -- local glyphs -- scoped to a single workflow.

- BlueprintBuilder gains local_glyphs: dict[str, str] = {} field
- Blueprint DB schema gets a local_glyphs JSON column
- merge_glyph_values moved from domain.run.compile to domain.glyphs.resolution
  and extended with a local_values parameter; resolution order is
  intrinsic < global < local < context (pinned keys preserved)
- validate_expand uses unified merge logic and validates that local
  glyph keys do not collide with intrinsic names (reported in global_errors)
- execute_background reconstructs builder with local_glyphs and passes
  them into merge_glyph_values
- Integration tests extended: expand test covers invalid/valid local glyph;
  execute test verifies local glyph value appears in workflow output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: consolidate Blueprint DB columns into single builder field

Replace the three separate JSON columns (blocks, environment_spec,
local_glyphs) with a single builder column that stores the full
BlueprintBuilder model dump. Simplifies save/load logic significantly:

- upsert_blueprint now takes a single builder: dict | None parameter
- load_builder uses BlueprintBuilder.model_validate() directly
- execute_background uses BlueprintBuilder.model_validate() directly
- run/service.py guards on blueprint.builder instead of blueprint.blocks
- Unit test updated to use new builder parameter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: validate blueprint at create/update; add validate_only flag

- validate_expand gains validate_only: bool = False parameter;
  when True, possible_sources and possible_expansions are skipped
  (saves expansion work when only error checking is needed), and the
  blueprint is deep-copied so that resolve_configurations mutations do
  not affect the caller's object.
  When False (default, expand endpoint), the blueprint may be mutated
  in place and expansion data is fully computed.
- /blueprint/create and /blueprint/update call validate_expand with
  validate_only=True and return 422 if any errors are present
  (global_errors or block_errors -- unknown plugins, undefined glyphs,
  config errors, intrinsic glyph key collisions, etc.)
- /blueprint/expand calls validate_expand with validate_only=False
- test_schedule._save_blueprint updated to use the test plugin instead
  of the unavailable ecmwf plugin so it passes the new validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [backend] type/import cleanups

commit.message:
feat(SM-15): Wire BindingAccumulator into processCallsFromExtracted for cross-file return type propagation (#763)

* Initial plan

* Initial setup - Phase 9 BindingAccumulator cross-file return type wiring

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/7cee6490-090d-4714-8cb5-a704168ff47a

* feat(SM-15): wire BindingAccumulator into processCallsFromExtracted for Phase 9 cross-file return type propagation

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/7cee6490-090d-4714-8cb5-a704168ff47a

* fix(SM-15): address all PR #763 review findings

Performance (R1)
- Changed _fileScopeByFile from Map<string, [string,string][]> to
  Map<string, Map<string,string>>. fileScopeGet(filePath, name) is
  now O(1) — replaces the O(n) linear scan + defensive-copy alloc
  that ran once per ConstructorBinding entry. fileScopeEntries()
  reconstructs tuples from Map.entries() for backward compat.
- Updated finalize() dev-mode invariant to compare deduplicated Map
  size rather than raw array length (Map.set deduplicates same-name).

Lifecycle (R2)
- Documented that Phase 9 intentionally reads pre-finalize because
  finalize() cannot move before both the worker consumer (line 984)
  AND the sequential-path writer (line 1061). Pre-finalize reads are
  safe because finalize() is write-lock-only with no side effects.
  Replaced the ambiguous "populated but not yet finalized" comment
  with the full lifecycle ordering explanation.

Sequential-path parity (R3)
- Wired bindingAccumulator into processCalls at line 797 (sequential
  path) so verifyConstructorBindings gets the Phase 9 fallback.
- Added bindingAccumulator parameter to processAssignmentsFromExtracted
  signature and wired it at the pipeline.ts call site (line 1026).
- Both paths now produce identical Phase 9 behavior for the same code.

Tracking comments (R4)
- Added "Overlapping mechanism (N of 3)" cross-references at:
  1. buildImportedReturnTypes (~line 109)
  2. collectExportedBindings (~line 168)
  3. Phase 9 fallback in verifyConstructorBindings (~line 563)
  Each links to the other two and notes future unification.

Language coverage (R5)
- Added 5 new Phase 9 integration test suites in cross-file-binding.test.ts:
  JavaScript, C++, C#, PHP, Ruby. Each uses the existing fixture
  directories and asserts getUser() → User → user.save() resolves.
  Total cross-file binding tests: 52 (was 37).

Quality asymmetry (R6)
- Added inline comment at the Phase 9 fallback noting worker-path
  entries are Tier 0/1 only and that binding accuracy is structurally
  lower for large repos where the worker path dominates.

Tests (+21 new)
- 6 fileScopeGet unit tests (happy path, unknown file/name, mixed
  scopes, post-dispose, duplicate varName last-write-wins)
- 15 integration tests across 5 new language suites

Verification
- tsc --noEmit clean
- 3147 unit tests pass (+6 new)
- 52 cross-file binding integration tests pass (+15 new)
- 1766 resolver integration tests pass
- Zero regressions

Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-10-001-fix-sm15-review-findings-plan.md
Review: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/pull/763#issuecomment-4220354242

* fix(SM-15): gate accumulator fallback on resolution tier and fix sequential file-order dependency

Two Codex adversarial reviews identified medium-severity bugs in the Phase 9
BindingAccumulator fallback:

1. Local-first violation: the fallback fired regardless of whether ctx.resolve()
   found same-file candidates, letting an imported callee shadow a local one
   and produce false CALLS edges. Fixed by gating on tiered.tier !== 'same-file'
   and callableDefs.length <= 1.

2. Sequential file-order dependency: processCalls flushed and verified per-file,
   so consumer files processed before their providers missed accumulator bindings.
   Fixed by splitting into a flush pre-pass (all files) then a resolution loop,
   mirroring the worker path's "all appends before any reads" pattern.

Also adds 11 consumer-before-provider integration test fixtures (one per
supported language) and 4 unit tests for tier gating edge cases.

* refactor(SM-15): eliminate duplicated prepare logic in processCalls two-pass split

Replace the duplicated pre-pass + legacy-path code (parse → query → heritage
→ TypeEnv → exports) with a single preparation loop followed by a resolution
loop. Both paths now share the same preparation code — the only conditional
is the accumulator flush.

Side benefit: globalParentMap is now fully populated before any resolution
runs, improving cross-file isSubclassOf accuracy regardless of file order.

Net -118 lines (226 removed, 108 added).

* fix(SM-15): address PR #763 third-pass review findings

1. Update stale dispose() JSDoc — remove forward-reference to Phase 9
   wiring that is now complete; document actual consumers.

2. Add processAssignmentsFromExtracted Phase 9 unit test — verifies the
   accumulator fallback produces ACCESSES write edges when the SymbolTable
   has no returnType for the callee.

commit.message:
Fix critical Jarvis startup issues discovered by codebase audit

Major fixes based on comprehensive investigation:

1. **OrbShellWindow inheritance bug (CRITICAL):**
   - Changed QMainWindow.__init__() to super().__init__()
   - This was causing AttributeError for _action_btn_css and other methods
   - Explains why OrbShellWindow crashed immediately on startup

2. **Conda re-execution loop prevention (HIGH):**
   - Added guard to prevent infinite re-execution loop
   - Set _JARVIS_GUI_REEXEC_ATTEMPTED env var to detect recursion
   - Prevents double-launching when venv/bin/python is conda python

3. **Multiple API instances prevention (HIGH):**
   - Added _API_STARTED guard flag to api.py
   - Prevents start() from creating multiple API instances on same/different ports
   - Fixes cascading instance spawning behavior

4. **Window cleanup improvements:**
   - Added multiprocessing child process cleanup in closeEvent
   - Ensures threads are properly terminated on shutdown

These fixes address the root causes of:
- Jarvis failing to open properly
- Multiple instances spawning when it does open
